clinical_coherence.notes: 65M with classic asymmetric right-sided resting tremor, cogwheel rigidity, bradykinesia, hypophonia, masked facies, micrographia, and gait changes. Vitals appropriate. Neuro exam is textbook early PD. Family history of father with PD adds plausibility.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Normal brain MRI (expected in PD), normal labs excluding secondary causes, DaTscan showing asymmetric putaminal loss R>L concordant with left-predominant clinical signs, robust 50% levodopa response, subclinical RBD on PSG. All perfectly aligned with idiopathic PD.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G20 correct. Differential is well-calibrated (ET, drug-induced, MSA, PSP, NPH) with appropriate likelihood ratings. Critical actions and contraindicated actions are clinically sound. Key reasoning points are accurate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic textbook PD presentation with unilateral onset, pill-rolling tremor, positive family history, and robust levodopa response. No diagnostic ambiguity. Perfectly calibrated as straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 65-year-old male with HTN, hyperlipidemia, BPH, and family history of PD. Highly realistic demographic profile for idiopathic PD.

educational_value.notes: Solid teaching case for classic PD recognition. Good breadth of workup. Slightly limited discriminative power as the diagnosis is very straightforward, but excellent for establishing baseline PD features.

strengths: Comprehensive followup outputs including DaTscan, levodopa challenge, autonomic testing, neuropsych, and PSG
